children.text: lands owned by an agency of the United States, other than land held in trust for Indian tribes;
lands owned in fee title by a State, including an agency or a subdivision of a State, or a unit of local government;
land subject to an easement or deed restriction which, as determined by the Secretary, provides similar protection as would be provided by enrollment in the program; or

lands where the purposes of the program would be undermined due to on-site or off-site conditions, such as risk of hazardous substances, permitted or existing rights of way, infrastructure development, or adjacent land uses.
in the case of an agricultural land easement, is grassland that would benefit from protection under a long-term easement; and

in the case of a wetland reserve easement, is a wetland or related area with the highest wetland functions and value and is likely to return to production after the land leaves the conservation reserve program.

children.heading: Subordination, exchange, modification, and termination
children.label: c Subordination, exchange, modification, and termination
A
children.text: increases conservation values or has a limited negative effect on conservation values;

children.label: B
children.text: minimally affects the acreage subject to the interest in land; and

children.label: C
children.text: is in the public interest or furthers the practical administration of the program.

children.label: i
children.text: no reasonable alternative exists and the effect on the interest in land is avoided or minimized to the extent practicable; and

children.label: I
children.text: results in equal or increased conservation values;

children.label: II
children.text: results in equal or greater economic value to the United States;

children.label: III
children.text: is consistent with the original intent of the easement;

children.label: IV
children.text: is consistent with the purposes of the program; and

children.label: V
children.text: is in the public interest or furthers the practical administration of the program.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: B Limitation
children.text: In modifying or exchanging an interest in land, or portion of such interest, under this paragraph, the Secretary may not increase any payment to an eligible entity.

children.label: A
children.text: termination is in the interest of the Federal Government;

children.label: i
children.text: the fair market value of the interest in land;

children.label: ii
children.text: any costs relating to the termination; and

children.label: iii
children.text: any damages determined appropriate by the Secretary; and

children.label: i
children.text: address a compelling public need for which there is no practicable alternative even with avoidance and minimization; and

children.label: ii
children.text: further the practical administration of the program.

children.heading: Consent
children.label: 4 Consent
children.text: The Secretary shall obtain consent from the landowner and eligible entity, if applicable, for any subordination, exchange, modification, or termination of interest in land, or portion of such interest, under this subsection.

children.heading: Notice
children.label: 5 Notice
children.text: At least 90 days before taking any termination action described in paragraph (3), the Secretary shall provide written notice of such action to the Committee on Agriculture of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry of the Senate.

children.heading: Land enrolled in other programs
children.label: d Land enrolled in other programs

children.heading: Conservation reserve program
children.label: 1 Conservation reserve program
children.text: The Secretary may terminate or modify a contract entered into under if eligible land that is subject to such contract is enrolled in an easement under .section 3831(a) of this titlesection 3865c(b) of this title

children.heading: Other
children.label: 2 Other
children.text: In accordance with the provisions of subtitle H of title II of the Agricultural Act of 2014, land enrolled in the wetlands reserve program, grassland reserve program, or farmland protection program on the day before , shall be considered enrolled in the program.February 7, 2014

children.heading: Agricultural land easements
children.label: 3 Agricultural land easements
children.text: A farmer or rancher who owns eligible land subject to an agricultural land easement may enter into a contract under subpart B of part I of subchapter IV.
to comply with applicable conservation requirements under subchapter II; and
to comply with applicable wetland protection requirements under subchapter III.
